slides.title: Time the Seizure
slides.content: Note the exact start time. A seizure lasting more than 5 minutes is status epilepticus — a life-threatening emergency. Call 911 if: first ever seizure, duration exceeds 5 minutes, person does not regain consciousness, injury occurred, or another seizure follows.
slides.warning: When in doubt, call 911. You cannot overdose on calling for help.
slides.tts_text: Note the start time now. Call 911 if the seizure lasts more than 5 minutes.

slides.title: Protect from Injury
slides.content: Clear the immediate area of hard or sharp objects. Cushion the head with something soft (folded jacket, bag). Remove glasses if worn. Loosen tight clothing around the neck.
slides.tts_text: Clear the area. Cushion the head. Remove glasses. Loosen any tight clothing around the neck.

slides.title: What NOT to Do
slides.content: Do NOT hold the person down or try to restrain their movements. Do NOT put anything in their mouth — they cannot swallow their tongue (this is a myth). Do NOT give water or food. Do NOT leave them alone.
slides.warning: Restraining a seizure can cause fractures or dislocations. Putting objects in the mouth risks broken teeth and injury to you both.
slides.tts_text: Do not restrain the person. Do not put anything in their mouth. Do not give food or water. Stay with them.

slides.title: Recovery Position When Shaking Stops
slides.content: Once convulsions stop: gently roll the person onto their side (recovery position). This keeps the airway clear and prevents aspiration if they vomit. Place top knee forward to stabilize.
slides.tts_text: Once shaking stops, gently roll the person onto their side. This keeps the airway clear.

slides.title: Check Breathing
slides.content: Confirm they are breathing normally. Look, listen, and feel. If not breathing after convulsions stop, begin CPR immediately.
slides.warning: Do not attempt rescue breathing during active convulsions — only after.
slides.tts_text: Check for normal breathing. If not breathing after the seizure ends, begin CPR.

slides.title: Recovery Phase
slides.content: Confusion and disorientation for 5–30 minutes after a seizure is normal (postictal phase). Speak calmly and reassure them. Tell them what happened. Do not offer food or water until fully alert. Do not allow them to drive.

completion.message: Stay calm. Stay with them through the recovery. Do not let them drive.

slides.tts_text: Speak calmly. Confusion after a seizure is normal. Stay until fully recovered. Do not let them drive.
